subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The International Space Station
description: One-minute skill: The International Space Station.

hook.text: The ISS is the largest structure humans have ever built in space—as big as a football field, orbiting at 28,000 km/h.

buildup.text: Fifteen nations collaborated to build it starting in 1998. It took over 40 missions to assemble in orbit.

discovery.text: Astronauts have lived aboard continuously since 2000. Over 3,000 experiments have been conducted in microgravity.

twist.text: It's visible to the naked eye—the third-brightest object in the night sky after the Moon and Venus.

climax.text: After 25 years of service, the ISS will be deorbited around 2030. New commercial stations are being planned to replace it.

punchline.text: Fifteen nations, one station. Humanity's home in orbit for 25 years.

quiz.question: How many nations collaborated to build the International Space Station?
